note: Paragraphs extracted from arXiv HTML version (https://arxiv.org/html/2605.29442v1) rather than LaTeX source, as no .tex source was publicly available. Reference below is a structured summary of the paper's section organization from the HTML rendering.

sections.content_brief: Motivation, gap analysis, contribution statement, overview of 4 RQs, highlight of key findings
2.1 Coding Agents in Developer Workflows, 2.2 Failure Analysis of Coding Agents, 2.3 Human-AI Alignment
3.1 Datasets (SpecStory + SWE-chat), 3.2 Structured Misalignment Extraction, 3.3 Post-Extraction Validation, 3.4 Multi-Axial Annotation
4.1 RQ1 (7 symptom + 7 cause categories with Table 2), 4.2 RQ2 (outcome/resolution with Table 3), 4.3 RQ3 (IDE vs CLI), 4.4 RQ4 (co-occurrence, cross-session persistence, temporal trends)
Interaction symptoms as alignment gap, safety implications of dependence on developer review, log-based behavioral signals as path forward
Selection bias, visibility restrictions, modality confounding, LLM-dependent annotation quality
Data ethics (public logs only, PII redaction), replication package at GitHub ND-SaNDwichLAB/coding-agent-misalignment

A.1 Post-extraction validation details (Table 4), A.2 Per-axis annotation quality (Table 5), B Within-episode label structure (Table 6), C Outcome analysis (Table 7), D Structural/temporal effects (Figures 5-6, Table 8), E Representative episodes (S1-S7 examples)
